2604.24158.title: Multi-Dimensional Evaluation of Sustainable City Trips with LLM-as-a-Judge and Human-in-the-Loop
2604.24158.paper_summary: Evaluating nuanced conversational travel recommendations is challenging when human annotations are costly and standard metrics ignore stakeholder-centric goals. We study LLMs-as-Judges for sustainable city-trip lists across four dimensions -- relevance, diversity, sustainability, and popularity balance, and propose a three-phase calibration framework: (1) baseline judging with multiple LLMs, (2) expert evaluation to identify systematic misalignment, and (3) dimension-specific calibration via rules and few-shot examples. Across two recommendation settings, we observe model-specific biases and high dimension-level variance, even when judges agree on overall rankings. Calibration clarifies reasoning per dimension but exposes divergent interpretations of sustainability, highlighting the need for transparent, bias-aware LLM evaluation. Prompts and code are released for reproducibility: https://github.com/ashmibanerjee/trs-llm-calibration.
2604.24158.paper_summary_zh: 評估細緻的對話旅行建議是具有挑戰性的，因為人類標註的成本高昂，而標準指標忽略了以利益相關者為中心的目標。我們研究了作為評審的 LLMs，針對可持續城市旅行清單的四個維度——相關性、多樣性、可持續性和受歡迎程度平衡，並提出了一個三階段的校準框架：(1) 使用多個 LLMs 進行基準評審，(2) 專家評估以識別系統性不對齊，以及 (3) 通過規則和少量示例進行維度特定的校準。在兩個推薦設定中，我們觀察到模型特定的偏見和高維度層次的變異，即使評審在整體排名上達成一致。校準澄清了每個維度的推理，但暴露了對可持續性的不同解釋，突顯了對透明且意識到偏見的 LLM 評估的需求。為了可重複性，已釋放提示和代碼：https://github.com/ashmibanerjee/trs-llm-calibration。
2604.24158.author: Ashmi Banerjee et.al.
2604.24158.authors: Ashmi Banerjee, Adithi Satish, Wolfgang Wörndl, Yashar Deldjoo
